div.p: To all who shall see or hear this written deed, Walter son of Alan, steward of the lord King of Scotland, sends greeting in the Lord. Let your whole community know me to have given and granted to God and to blessed Mary at Melrose and to the monks serving God in that place the whole land of Mow by its correct boundaries and whatever is contained within the same boundaries with all its pertinents which I held in feu of the lord William de Vescy and all rights and public functions which applied to me or could apply by reason of the same or in the same, in free, pure, and perpetual alms to have, possess and hold for ever, as freely, quitely, fully and honourably as any alms in the kingdom of Scotland is freely, quitely, fully and honourably held and possessed. Neither I nor my heirs shall in the future claim back any right in the same land or in the things contained within the boundaires of the same land for ever; but we shall warrant, defend, and maintain against all people for ever to the same monks with everything mentioned before, free from every exaction, service, custom and whatever other service. As witness of this matter I have secured the present written deed by the application of my seal. With these witnesses: Thomas Crook, Simon son of Bertolf, Adam Wallace, William of Halkerston, Robert of Kippo, knights; William Broun, master of the nuns of South Berwick; William and Robert our chaplains; Leon de Normanville, Richard of Musselburgh, and John of Leicester, monks of Melrose; and many others.
